destinations.evidence: Parent-state scheduler obligation computation derives the due index interval from start_block/frequency/ttl/num_calls, skips frequency 0, supports frequency 1 as every block, and uses is_executed to prevent repeat firing of an index.

equivalence_argument: Mapped in the port replacement path: parent-state due-window computation is indexed by start_block/frequency/num_calls and execution bitmap checks enforce one-shot behavior per index.
rationale: The frequency/window candidates evolve from next-block selection to indexed execution. The frequency==1 one-time candidate is explicitly superseded by the later every-block candidate, and start_block/num_calls supersede initial_block-only wording.
statement: The scheduler queues or fires calls only when the candidate block is inside the call window and matches the current start_block/frequency schedule; frequency == 1 is recurring every block, and fired_count/current_index gating prevents more than one scheduled transaction per execution index.
suggested_falsification_tests: For frequency=1, assert each block in the TTL-bounded due interval produces the corresponding index once.
Mark an index executed and assert compute_obligations skips it.
summary: Canonical scheduled execution is windowed, indexed, and one-shot per index in the parent-state path.
test_or_falsification_candidate: Due-index bounds and execution bitmap regression tests.
title: Canonical scheduled execution is windowed, indexed, and one-shot per index
